rulings.Case Title: JEAN MARIE REED ET AL VS. NANCY LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 7, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT NANCY LEE, NAM SHI LEE, THE LEE REVOCABLE TRUST MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Hearing Required to address Na'il Benjamin's failure to appear at 11:00 a.m. on February 5, 2018 per Court January 26, 2018 Order. Na'il Benjamin is ordered to appear at 9:30 a.m. on February 6, 2018. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant's argument that Defendant is entitled to a summary judgment on Plaintiff's complaint as a matter of law because plaintiffs are unable to establish that Defendants breached any duty (Motion 1:5-28; 2:16-9:1) does not dispose of all causes of action in the First Amended Complaint . Motion for Summary Adjudication on issues 1 to 4 is granted. Defendants shifted their burden and Plaintiff failed to create a material triable issue of fact on any of the issues presented. As to the first issue, Plaintiff fails to present any evidence that the landlord was in violation of CCP 1942.4, specifically that the landlord demanded/collected rent while a notification of habitability issues made by a public officer was outstanding for over 35 days. As to the second issue, Plaintiff argues that there is a triable issue of fact as to when Plaintiff knew of should have known that the mold was a substantial factor in causing her bronchial infection, no evidence is cited to indicate that the date of accrual was later than July 2013. The Court notes that opposition as to issues 3 and 4 is limited to 5 lines each and is devoid of any citations to facts or authority. The Court further notes that Plaintiffs do not assert that an affidavit required under CCP 377.32 has been filed. CCP 437c(h) request is DENIED. Plaintiff fails to identify what essential facts pertaining to issues 1-4 may be obtained at Defendant's opposition and why those facts have not been timely obtained. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER VARGAS VS. BARBARA FEDUN ET AL

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. INA JOAN KIM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Against Defendant Ina Joan Kim
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 7, 2018, Line 6. PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Against Defendant Ina Joan Kim. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Motion for Summary judgment based on Defendant using the premises for a purpose other than that of residential dwelling and violation of Administrative Code 41A has been previously heard and denied. Motion for Summary Adjudication of Affirmative defenses at issue in this motion has been previously heard and GRANTED. Plaintiff fails to provide any argument or authority for this Court to hear either the Motion for Summary Judgment or the Motion for Summary Adjudication again. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLY RHINE GAGOSIAN VS. LYDIA NORMAND RHINE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 7, 2018, Line 7. DEFENDANT LYDIA RHINE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. First Amended Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on termination of license and the explanation for the use of term "landlord" in the Notice is sufficient; the use of wrong terminology does not create a tenancy. The Court however notes that Plaintiff's use of wrong terminology such as "landlord" instead of "licensor" and "tenancy" instead of "occupancy" may be confusing. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND, LLC VS. CATHERINE HERRERA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Quash Service; Reply To Opposition To Moiton Quash; Memorandum Of Points Of Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 7, 2018, Line 8. DEFENDANT CATHERINE HERRERA'S Motion To Quash Service of summons. DENIED. Defendant served by Posting and Mailing pursuant to Court Order. Defendant fails to Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that otherwise valid serve is ineffective due to the "personal service" box being checked on the Summons and Complaint. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: IMBELLONI CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. VS. UNDRA MITCHELL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 7, 2018, Line 9. DEFENDANT UNDRA MITCHELL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile allegations in the Complaint (paragraph 6(d) rent "$1,036.62 effective May 1, 2016") and Three-Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit (demanding rent in October 2017 of "1,036.62" and "$100"). =(501/REQ)
